synthesis_text: COVID-19 is mainly transmitted from person to person through inhaling air contaminated by droplets and aerosols containing the virus. Infected individuals exhale these particles when they breathe, talk, cough, sneeze, or sing. Transmission is most likely at close range but can occur over longer distances, especially indoors. Additionally, COVID-19 can spread when infectious particles come into contact with the eyes, nose, or mouth. Small airborne particles can remain suspended in the air and travel longer distances indoors. Touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can also lead to transmission. People can spread the virus even if they do not develop symptoms, remaining contagious for up to 20 days.

COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through respiratory droplets. The virus can be transmitted when an infected person coughs, sneezes, talks, or breathes, releasing droplets that may land on nearby individuals or be inhaled by them [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. Close contact with an infected person increases the risk of transmission, especially in areas with low airflow [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00008]. Additionally, it is possible to contract COVID-19 from someone who shows no symptoms [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00007]. Touching surfaces contaminated with the virus and then touching the face can also lead to transmission [doc1_covid19.txt:chunk_00004].

COVID-19 spreads mainly from person to person through respiratory droplets that can be expelled from an infected person's mouth or nose when they cough, sneeze, speak, sing, or breathe. These droplets can range in size from larger respiratory droplets to smaller aerosols. People can be contagious whether or not they are displaying symptoms, and infection can also occur by touching the mouth, nose, or eyes after touching contaminated surfaces. Additionally, spending time in crowded spaces with poor airflow increases the risk of transmission [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00003][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00005][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00000][doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012].
